title: Implement opt-in local AW session logging

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Implement GitHub #2122's first narrow slice: opt-in local AW session logging for ordinary AW CLI invocations.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep logs under .agentic-workspace/local/, keep logging opt-in and non-authoritative, preserve generated CLI parser ownership, and keep logging failures non-fatal.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Implementation details for local session pointer, markdown log format, large-output artifact threshold, optional note command, and focused tests.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Validate the implemented session logging slice, reconcile acceptance, and close out with proof.
proof_expectations: uv run python scripts/run_agentic_workspace.py summary --target . --format json
uv run python scripts/run_agentic_workspace.py doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
touched_scope: .agentic-workspace payload sync and planning ownership files
completion_criteria: Local [session_logging] enabled = true is accepted from .agentic-workspace/config.local.toml without repo-owned policy changes.
Enabled logging creates/reuses one local session log under .agentic-workspace/local/logs/ with a stable current pointer and config prelude.
AW command stdout/stderr, normalized command input, target context, and exit status are appended, with large output recoverable from local artifacts.
agentic-workspace log note can append optional short notes without making notes mandatory.
Logging disabled and logging failures do not block ordinary AW commands.

goal: Implement GitHub #2122's first narrow slice: local opt-in AW session logging for command I/O, config prelude, optional notes, and local-only artifacts.
non_goals: Do not add remote telemetry or upload.
Do not log non-AW shell commands automatically.
Do not treat logs as Planning, Memory, proof receipts, or closeout authorization.
Do not move generated CLI parser/interface ownership into handwritten code beyond the source facade wrapper.

intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Add opt-in local AW session logging that captures AW command I/O and optional notes without changing repo-owned policy.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Source CLI facade wraps generated command execution with best-effort local logging when [session_logging] enabled = true.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.max changed files: About 10 files, excluding generated caches and local ignored logs.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_session_logging.py -q; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; AW summary and planning doctor.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before changing generated command interface contracts or adding non-local telemetry.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Generated CLI parser files, remote telemetry, checked-in logs, and non-AW shell command capture.

completion_criteria: Opt-in local config enables session logging without changing repo-owned policy config.
One session log is created/reused under .agentic-workspace/local/logs/ and starts with an effective config/logging prelude.
AW command input/output, target context, and exit status are appended; large output remains recoverable from local artifact files.
Optional notes can be appended cheaply.
Disabled logging and logging failures do not block ordinary AW command behavior.

execution_run.what happened: Implemented opt-in local Agentic Workspace session logging for generated AW command invocations and notes.
execution_run.scope touched: Source CLI facade, local config parsing, local session log writer, behavior tests, and AW planning state.

finished_run_review.scope respected: Implementation satisfies issue #2122 acceptance for opt-in local-only logs while preserving generated CLI command ownership and non-authoritative proof boundaries.
